rewrites.q: In hospitality data (venue bookings, shift reports), what's the classic timezone gotcha that produces wrong-day reports?
rewrites.a:
A venue's **Saturday night** crosses UTC midnight, so naive day-of-week filtering on UTC timestamps splits a single service across two days.

**Rule:** always convert to **venue-local time** before bucketing by day-of-week or business-day.

- Store timestamps as UTC `timestamptz`
- Attach the venue's IANA zone (e.g. `America/New_York`) to every venue row
- Compute `local_date` at query time, not at write time (DST changes)
- A **business day** often ends at 4am local, not midnight

rewrites.q: What's the standard cold-start playbook for launching a two-sided marketplace?
rewrites.a:
Two-sided liquidity is the single hardest marketplace problem, so the playbook is to **collapse it into a one-sided problem first**.

- **Pre-seed one side** manually (recruit suppliers door-to-door, or fake demand with concierge ops)
- **Drive demand to that seeded side** in a single concentrated geo, not nationally
- **Prove utility in one metro** (NYC, SF) before expanding
- **Expand city-by-city**, repeating the seed-then-demand loop

**Implication:** spend on supply acquisition early; spend on demand only once supply density clears a usability threshold.

rewrites.q: When deploying an AI product into a hospital, what's the biggest non-technical hurdle and how does it shape your architecture?
rewrites.a:
The hospital's **security and compliance review**, typically **8 to 16 weeks**, gates every deployment regardless of code quality.

- Bake the review window into your project timeline from day one
- Multi-tenant SaaS is usually **DOA** with hospital infosec
- Standard pattern is **single-tenant in the customer's VPC** (or on-prem)
- Expect questionnaires (HECVAT, SIG), pen-test reports, and a signed **BAA**

**Implication:** your deploy story (Terraform into customer cloud, air-gap mode) is part of the product, not an afterthought.

rewrites.q: What is a HIPAA Business Associate Agreement (BAA) and when does your company need one?
rewrites.a:
A **BAA** is a contract required whenever a vendor handles **PHI** (protected health information) on a covered entity's behalf. It binds the vendor to HIPAA's privacy and security rules and assigns breach-notification duties.

- **No BAA == no PHI** can legally flow to you
- Covered entities = providers, payers, clearinghouses; their vendors are **business associates**
- Most cloud and LLM vendors (AWS, GCP, Azure, OpenAI, Anthropic) offer BAAs only on **specific (often higher-tier) plans**
- Confirm BAA coverage for **every** subprocessor in your stack before architecting

rewrites.q: What infrastructure and personnel requirements does a defense or federal-government FDE deployment add on top of a normal enterprise SaaS deploy?
rewrites.a:
Public-sector workloads layer **three hard requirements** on top of commercial deployments.

- **Air-gapped on-prem deploys** (no outbound internet; updates via approved media)
- **FedRAMP High** for civilian agencies, **DoD IL5** (or IL6 for classified) for defense
- **Cleared engineers** (Secret / TS / TS-SCI) on the deploy and support path

**Implication:** procurement cycles are **months to years**, contracts are vehicle-based (GSA, OTA, SBIR), and your build/release pipeline needs a separate sovereign-cloud variant.

rewrites.q: What are the non-negotiable properties of a production fintech ledger?
rewrites.a:
A correct ledger is **double-entry, append-only, and idempotent**. These three properties are load-bearing for audit and dispute resolution.

- **Double-entry:** every transaction posts equal-and-opposite debits and credits; balances are derived, never stored as truth
- **Append-only:** never `UPDATE` or `DELETE`; corrections are **offsetting entries** (reversal + new posting)
- **Idempotent writes:** every API call carries an idempotency key so retries can't double-post
- **Audit trail is sacred:** regulators and disputes both replay history from the log

rewrites.q: What's the core UX and infrastructure challenge unique to multimodal generative AI (image, video, audio synthesis)?
rewrites.a:
Inference takes **seconds to minutes**, not milliseconds, so synchronous request/response breaks down and the whole stack shifts to a **long-running-job pattern**.

- **Async job queue** with durable job IDs (so refresh/disconnect is safe)
- **Progress channel:** WebSocket push or client polling against a status endpoint
- **Content moderation pre-gen** (prompt) and **post-gen** (output)
- **Cancellation tokens** so users can kill expensive in-flight jobs and free GPU
- Aggressive **caching** of identical prompts and **resumable** multi-step pipelines

rewrites.q: What's the hardest UX problem in real-time voice AI today, and what's the current target latency?
rewrites.a:
**Turn-taking and barge-in** (letting the user interrupt the model) are the hardest problems. Naive systems either talk over the user or leave awkward pauses.

- Industry target is **sub-500ms end-to-end** via streaming **STT to LLM to TTS** overlap
- **Neural turn-prediction** models have replaced VAD-only silence detection
- Server-side **interruption handling** must cut TTS audio within ~100ms of detected speech
- Low latency is **necessary but not sufficient**: natural turn boundaries are what make it feel human

rewrites.q: Between PLG (product-led growth) and SLG (sales-led growth) motions, which one actually needs Forward Deployed Engineers and why?
rewrites.a:
**SLG** needs FDEs; **PLG** generally does not.

**SLG (sales-led, enterprise contracts)**
- Deals are 6- to 7-figure, deployments are bespoke
- FDE is the **customer-engineering arm**: integrations, custom workflows, on-site enablement
- Without FDE, sold deals stall in pilot purgatory

**PLG (self-serve, bottom-up adoption)**
- Onboarding is the product; docs and DX replace humans
- Support is community + tiered CSMs, not embedded engineers

**Implication:** Palantir, Scale, Anthropic-enterprise = FDE-heavy; Linear, Vercel = PLG-light.

rewrites.q: In marketplace matching, when do you use greedy ranking versus ML ranking, and why does pure greedy fail?
rewrites.a:
Pure **greedy by rating** maximizes short-term match quality but **starves new entrants**: they never accumulate ratings, so the supply pool calcifies and eventually shrinks.

- **Greedy:** simple, explainable, good for early-stage when liquidity is low
- **Bipartite (Hungarian, Gale-Shapley):** when matches are scarce and global optimality matters (ride-share dispatch, kidney exchange)
- **ML rank with diversity injection** (5 to 10% traffic routed to newer or lower-ranked suppliers) keeps the supply side healthy and discovers latent winners

**Implication:** treat exploration as a fixed cost of marketplace health, not a leak.

rewrites.q: When designing US money-movement, when do you pick ACH vs RTP/FedNow vs wire?
rewrites.a:
**ACH**
- Cost: **cents** per transfer; settles in **1-3 business days** (batch)
- **Reversible** within ~60 days
- Best for **payroll, recurring debits, B2C disbursements**

**RTP / FedNow**
- **Instant** (seconds), **24/7/365**, **irrevocable**
- Capped (currently **$1M** on RTP)
- Best for **payouts, just-in-time funding, instant refunds**

**Wire (Fedwire, CHIPS)**
- **Same-day**, irrevocable, **$15-30** fee
- Best for **high-value B2B, real estate, treasury**

**Implication:** speed and irreversibility are the levers; cheaper rails trade settlement time for cost.

rewrites.q: What's the cheapest practical way to stay PCI-DSS compliant when accepting card payments?
rewrites.a:
**Never let raw PAN (the card number) touch your servers.** Scope is everything in PCI: the less you touch, the easier the audit.

- Use a **tokenization vendor** (Stripe Elements, Spreedly, Basis Theory) via **iframe or hosted SDK**
- Your servers see only an opaque token, never the PAN or CVV
- This qualifies you for **SAQ-A**, the lightest self-assessment (essentially a checklist)
- Touching PAN in plaintext pushes you into **SAQ-D** + full QSA audit + quarterly scans

**Implication:** architecture choice on day one determines whether compliance costs $0 or $100K+/year.

rewrites.q: In fintech onboarding, what's the practical difference between KYC and KYB?
rewrites.a:
**KYC (Know Your Customer)**
- Verifies an **individual**
- Inputs: government ID, selfie liveness, SSN, address
- Screens: OFAC/sanctions, PEP, adverse media
- Mostly **automated** (Persona, Alloy, Socure); minutes to verify

**KYB (Know Your Business)**
- Verifies a **business entity**
- Inputs: EIN, formation docs, operating agreement
- Requires **UBO** (ultimate beneficial owners, >25% ownership) discovery, then KYC on each UBO
- Heavily **manual review**, document chasing; days to weeks

**Implication:** KYB is the bottleneck for B2B fintech onboarding; budget for ops headcount, not just software.

rewrites.q: For new healthcare integrations in 2026, when do you use FHIR vs HL7 v2?
rewrites.a:
**FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources)**
- **REST / JSON**, resource-oriented (`Patient`, `Observation`, `MedicationRequest`)
- Modern standard; **mandated for US patient-access APIs** under ONC/CMS rules
- Used for **greenfield apps**, patient portals, third-party integrations

**HL7 v2**
- Pipe-delimited messages over **MLLP/TCP**
- Still **dominant inside hospital walls** for ADT (admit/discharge/transfer), orders, lab results
- Required when integrating with legacy EHRs (Epic, Cerner via interface engines)

**Implication:** real production systems speak **both**: FHIR at the edge, HL7 v2 internally via a Mirth/Rhapsody engine.

rewrites.q: Why is **DAU/MAU** a misleading north-star metric for developer tools, and what should you use instead?
rewrites.a:
Developer tools are **workflow tools, not consumer apps**. A developer using your CLI three days a week is highly engaged, but DAU/MAU would score them at 0.43 and look bad.

Better north stars:
- **Integration depth:** commands run per active dev, languages/repos covered, % of workflow replaced
- **Retained weekly active devs (WAU)** with cohort retention, not raw daily
- **Time-to-first-value (TTFV):** signup -> first successful API call / build / deploy
- **Paid seat expansion** within an org (the real PLG growth loop)

**Implication:** optimizing for DAU pushes you toward notifications and engagement bait, which devs hate.

rewrites.q: What is "eval gating" at AI-first companies (Anthropic, OpenAI, Deepgram) and why is it considered table-stakes?
rewrites.a:
**Eval gating** means treating evaluations as **CI for prompts and models**. Every change must pass a frozen golden-set with regression thresholds before merge.

- **Frozen golden-set** of input/output pairs; thresholds per metric (accuracy, latency, safety)
- **Prompt versioning:** prompts are code, stored in git, addressable by hash
- **Shadow traffic** in prod: new prompt/model runs alongside old, deltas logged before cutover
- **Per-PR eval runs** + dashboards in code review

**Implication:** without eval gating, every prompt edit silently regresses quality and you only find out from customer complaints.

rewrites.q: Why do production AI apps need a model router, and how much does it actually save?
rewrites.a:
**Mechanism:** different requests have different cost/latency/quality needs. A router classifies the request (intent, length, sensitivity) and dispatches to the **cheapest model that meets the SLO**.

- **Haiku / small models:** classification, extraction, routing decisions
- **Sonnet / mid models:** chat, summarization, most agent steps
- **Opus / frontier:** hard reasoning, long-horizon planning, code review
- Routing signal can be heuristic (token count, regex) or a tiny classifier

**Implication:** cuts inference cost **5-10x** versus always-Opus with negligible quality loss, and gives you a graceful **fallback ladder** when a provider degrades.

rewrites.q: In hospitality, why does **RevPAR** beat raw occupancy as a north-star metric?
rewrites.a:
**RevPAR (revenue per available room) = ADR x occupancy**, so it captures the price-vs-fill tradeoff that single metrics miss.

- **Occupancy alone** rewards **fire-sale discounting**: fill rooms at any price
- **ADR alone** (average daily rate) rewards **empty rooms** at premium prices
- **RevPAR** forces the yield-management tradeoff and is the **industry-standard** comp metric
- Restaurants have an analog: **RevPASH** (revenue per available seat-hour)

**Implication:** when you see hotels heavily discounting on OTAs, it's almost always a RevPAR play, not a desperate occupancy grab.

rewrites.q: Why is integrating with restaurant POS systems (Toast, Square, Aloha, Micros) harder than typical SaaS API integration?
rewrites.a:
**Three structural problems** make restaurant POS integration painful even when an API exists.

- **Inconsistent menu modeling:** modifiers, combos, voids, comps, and 86'd items are represented differently in every POS
- **Partial real-time APIs:** Toast and Square are reasonable; **Aloha and Micros often require polling or on-prem agents** because the POS is a Windows box in the back office
- **Ticket-time semantics vary:** "fired," "served," "closed" mean different things per venue and per POS

**Implication:** expect to write **per-POS adapters**, reconcile menu state **nightly**, and maintain a canonical menu model your app actually queries.

rewrites.q: What core features must an enterprise LLM gateway (Credal, Portkey, internal builds) provide, and who is the buyer?
rewrites.a:
**Mechanism:** the gateway sits between internal apps and LLM providers as a single chokepoint for security, cost, and observability.

- **Centralized API keys** with **per-team budgets and rate limits**
- **PII redaction on egress** before prompts hit external providers
- **Prompt/response logging** for audit and incident response
- **Model abstraction:** swap providers (OpenAI to Anthropic to Bedrock) without app changes
- **SSO + RBAC** so access follows employee status

**Implication:** the buyer is **security/IT, not developers**. The pitch is observability and a kill switch, and DX is secondary to control.
